findings.title: Base fork and fixture tests do not execute any launch-blocking routes
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the fork-smoke test and zero-count manifest assertions with manifest-driven tests that require a nonzero pinned Base block, validate every pool fixture, and execute every launch-blocking buy/sell route, including split, multi-hop, native V4 ETH, and failed-route atomicity cases.

findings.title: Adapter behavior is only tested through one generic mock executor
findings.suggested_fix: Add adapter-specific tests or fork tests that exercise Solidly Route[] stable flags, Uniswap V2 paths, Slipstream and Slipstream2 tick/router metadata, and Universal Router V3/V4 structured payloads. Include negative tests for malformed per-venue payloads and disallowed command/payer/recipient/sweep/wrap/unwrap/Permit2 semantics.

findings.title: Native ETH coverage misses intermediate ETH and WETH-distinction settlement
findings.suggested_fix: Add multi-leg tests where a later leg consumes native ETH produced by an earlier leg, plus cases with ambient ETH and ambient WETH present to prove native leftovers, WETH leftovers, final native output fees, and WETH ERC-20 accounting remain distinct.

findings.title: Approval cleanup and non-standard ERC20 paths are untested
findings.suggested_fix: Add token mocks for approve-to-zero failure, false/no-return approve or transferFrom, fee-on-transfer/deflationary input, malformed balanceOf, and reentrant transfer behavior. Assert allowances are cleared on success and that cleanup or transfer failures revert atomically without stranded route-local funds or unexpected allowances.

findings.title: Deployment validation script has no executable test coverage
findings.suggested_fix: Add script tests or a validation harness covering wrong fee recipient, wrong fee ppm, missing router/FAME env, skip-NFT disabled, pending fixture snapshot, and the eventual launchable manifest success path before treating the script as a launch gate.

residual_risks: The tests intentionally document that the frozen production fixture snapshot is pending, so they are useful as a no-go signal but not proof of launch readiness.
MockRouter-based tests prove core balance accounting under ideal ERC20 behavior, but not compatibility with real Base venues, real token quirks, or Universal Router/V4 hook semantics.
No tests currently demonstrate FAME DN404 skip-NFT behavior during actual FAME custody; only the validation script checks a deployed address after the fact.
testing_gaps: Per-venue payload validation for Solidly, Uniswap V2, Slipstream, Slipstream2, Universal Router V3, and Universal Router V4.
Pinned Base fork execution for every manifest route and pool, including buy/sell coverage per venue family.
Native ETH as an intermediate route-local asset and native ETH/WETH leftover separation.
Approval cleanup failure handling and non-standard or malicious ERC20 behavior.
Deployment validation harness and live metadata/fixture parity checks.
